project_summary:
Centralized monitoring dashboard for multiple Aitri-managed projects. Provides a single-pane-of-glass view of pipeline progress, test health, Git activity, and alerts across N projects — via a React web dashboard served by a local Node.js process (Docker optional) at localhost:3000.

Product Analysis:
  North Star KPI: Time-to-insight — user sees all project statuses in ≤5 seconds from opening localhost:3000.
  JTBD: 'When I have multiple Aitri projects running in parallel, help me instantly know which ones need attention without cd-ing into each repo.'
  Guardrail metric: Dashboard render time must stay ≤2s for up to 20 registered projects.
no_go_zone: No cloud storage or remote sync — all state lives locally under ~/.aitri-hub/; no external API calls to persist data
No authentication or user accounts — no login, no sessions, no multi-user access control in v1
No aitri command execution from the dashboard — cannot trigger run-phase, approve, reject, or verify from UI or CLI monitor
No monitoring of non-Aitri projects — projects without a .aitri state file are not supported
No code editing or test execution — dashboard is read-only; it observes, never modifies project files
No mobile native build — web interface is browser-only via localhost; no iOS/Android app

user_personas.goal: See the health of all personal Aitri projects in one terminal command each morning without switching directories
user_personas.pain_point: Runs aitri status in each project folder sequentially — slow, no cross-project comparison, easy to miss a failing project

user_personas.goal: Share a live web dashboard URL with the team so everyone sees project statuses without needing CLI access
user_personas.pain_point: Must manually aggregate status from each developer's project, no single source of truth for the team

user_personas.goal: Monitor company-wide Aitri projects (local + GitHub) from a visual web dashboard without deep CLI knowledge
user_personas.pain_point: No visibility into pipeline health across teams; relies on manual status updates in Slack or email

functional_requirements.title: Project registration via browser admin UI
functional_requirements.description: User registers N projects (local paths, GitHub/GitLab URLs, or bulk folder scans) through the browser admin UI at localhost:3000/admin. The UI calls the admin HTTP API (POST /api/projects) which persists to ~/.aitri-hub/projects.json. The admin API is guarded localhost-only (loopback peer check) — no auth required, but remote peers are rejected with 403.
acceptance_criteria: /admin renders a form allowing single-project add, bulk folder scan, and remove actions
POST /api/projects with type='local' and a valid on-disk directory returns 201 and appends a new entry to ~/.aitri-hub/projects.json
POST /api/projects with type='remote' and a GitHub/GitLab URL stores the URL as-is without cloning
POST /api/projects with type='folder' scans child directories and adds each that contains package.json or .aitri/
DELETE /api/projects/:id removes the entry and returns 204
Non-loopback peers (not 127.0.0.1 / ::1) calling any /api/* route receive HTTP 403
Persisted projects.json remains valid JSON across crashes (atomic write via temp file + rename)

functional_requirements.title: Read and parse .aitri state file per project
functional_requirements.description: For each registered project, the system reads the .aitri state file and extracts: currentPhase, approvedPhases, completedPhases, verifyPassed, verifySummary, rejections, and artifactHashes (for drift detection).
acceptance_criteria: Given a project with a valid .aitri file, when the collector runs, then currentPhase, approvedPhases, and completedPhases are correctly extracted
Given a project with verifyPassed: true and a verifySummary, when collected, then passed/failed/skipped counts are available for display
Given a project where an artifact hash has changed post-approval (drift), when collected, then a drift flag is set to true for that project
Given a project whose .aitri file is missing or malformed, when collected, then the project entry shows status: 'unreadable' and no crash occurs
Given a project with rejections entries, when collected, then the most recent rejection timestamp and feedback string are extracted

functional_requirements.title: Collect Git metadata per project
functional_requirements.description: For each registered project, collect: last commit timestamp, commits in the last 7 days (velocity), and active branch name.
acceptance_criteria: Given a local project with git history, when collected, then last commit timestamp is accurate to within 1 minute of `git log -1 --format=%cd`
Given a local project, when collected, then commit velocity equals the count of commits in the last 7 calendar days
Given a project with no commits in ≥72 hours, when alerts are evaluated, then a 'stale' alert is generated for that project
Given a project that is not a git repository, when collected, then git fields are null and no crash occurs

functional_requirements.title: Read test results from 04_TEST_RESULTS.json
functional_requirements.description: For each registered project, read spec/04_TEST_RESULTS.json (when present) to extract per-FR coverage status and summary counts (passed, failed, skipped, total).
acceptance_criteria: Given a project with a valid 04_TEST_RESULTS.json, when collected, then summary.passed, summary.failed, summary.total are correctly extracted
Given a project with 04_TEST_RESULTS.json containing fr_coverage, when collected, then each FR's coverage status ('covered'|'partial'|'uncovered') is available
Given a project where 04_TEST_RESULTS.json is absent, when collected, then test fields are null and display shows 'Tests: N/A'
Given a project with summary.failed > 0, when alerts are evaluated, then a 'tests failing' alert is generated

functional_requirements.title: Browser-first empty state when no projects are registered
functional_requirements.description: When projects.json is empty (or missing), the browser dashboard at localhost:3000 renders an onboarding empty state instead of a broken grid or CLI wizard prompt. The empty state has a single primary CTA that routes the user to /admin to register their first project.
acceptance_criteria: GET / with zero projects shows an onboarding panel with title 'No projects yet', descriptive body copy, and a primary CTA 'Add your first project'
Clicking the CTA navigates to /admin and the registration form is visible on arrival
The empty state includes a disclosure 'What counts as a project?' explaining .aitri/ directory and Aitri snapshot detection
No terminal-wizard or CLI-command text appears anywhere in the empty-state copy
Layout of the empty state fits within a single viewport at ≥768px height without vertical scroll

functional_requirements.title: Web dashboard — React served by the Node process at localhost:3000
functional_requirements.description: Provide a React web dashboard accessible at localhost:3000 via `aitri-hub web` (primary) or `docker compose up` (optional packaged deployment). Renders all registered projects with auto-refresh.
acceptance_criteria: Running `aitri-hub web` makes the dashboard accessible at http://localhost:3000 within 5 seconds on a machine with Node.js 18+ installed (Docker path is optional and may take longer on first build)
Dashboard renders all registered projects within ≤2 seconds of page load for up to 20 projects
Each project card displays: name, phase progress, verify pass/fail counts, last commit timestamp, active branch, and alerts
Dashboard auto-refreshes every 5 seconds without a full page reload
Layout is usable at viewport widths ≥768px without horizontal scroll
Alert badges are visually distinct — minimum contrast ratio of 4.5:1 against card background

functional_requirements.title: Smart alerts engine
functional_requirements.description: Evaluate collected project data against alert rules and surface actionable warnings: stale project (no commits >72h), verify failed, artifact drift detected, tests failing.
acceptance_criteria: Given a project with last commit >72 hours ago, then alert 'No commits in Xh' is generated
Given a project with verifySummary.failed > 0 or verifyPassed: false, then alert 'Verify failed' is generated
Given a project with at least one artifact drift flag, then alert 'Artifact drift detected' is generated
Given a project with 04_TEST_RESULTS.json where summary.failed > 0, then alert 'Tests failing (N)' is generated
Given a project with no alert conditions, then no alerts are shown for that project
Alert evaluation completes within ≤500ms per project

functional_requirements.title: Remote project support (GitHub/GitLab URLs)
functional_requirements.description: For projects registered with a remote URL, clone the repo to a local cache directory, read its .aitri state and spec/ artifacts, and refresh the cache on each monitor cycle.
acceptance_criteria: Given a registered GitHub URL, when monitor runs for the first time, then the repo is cloned to ~/.aitri-hub/cache/<repo-name>/
Given a cached remote project, when monitor refreshes, then `git pull` is run on the cached clone to fetch latest state
Given a remote project that cannot be reached (network error), when monitor runs, then the project shows last known cached data with a 'Cache stale' label and no crash occurs
Cache directory is reused across monitor sessions — no re-clone if cache exists

functional_requirements.title: Consolidated dashboard data file
functional_requirements.description: After each collection cycle, write aggregated project data to ~/.aitri-hub/dashboard.json for consumption by the web dashboard API.
acceptance_criteria: dashboard.json is written after every collection cycle (every 5 seconds during monitor)
dashboard.json contains an array of project entries, each with: name, location, phases, verifyStatus, gitMeta, testSummary, alerts
dashboard.json is valid JSON — malformed writes do not corrupt the file (atomic write via temp file + rename)
Web dashboard reads dashboard.json as its data source; no direct project file access from the React frontend

user_stories.i_want: to register all my Aitri projects from the browser admin UI
user_stories.so_that: I can add, remove, and bulk-scan projects visually without memorizing CLI flags

acceptance_criteria.given: the user has 3 local Aitri projects and opens localhost:3000/admin
acceptance_criteria.when: they submit the add-project form 3 times (or use bulk folder scan once)
acceptance_criteria.then: ~/.aitri-hub/projects.json contains 3 valid entries and each appears in the dashboard grid without a page reload

user_stories.i_want: the dashboard to read each project's .aitri state file
user_stories.so_that: I can see the current pipeline phase and approval status without running aitri status manually

acceptance_criteria.given: a registered project at /home/user/projects/finance with .aitri containing approvedPhases:[1,2,3] and currentPhase:4
acceptance_criteria.then: the dashboard shows '3 phases approved, current phase: 4' for that project

acceptance_criteria.given: a registered project whose .aitri file has been deleted
acceptance_criteria.then: the project card shows status 'unreadable' and all other projects continue to display normally

user_stories.i_want: to see which projects have had no recent commits
user_stories.so_that: I can follow up with developers on stagnant work before it becomes a blocker

acceptance_criteria.given: a registered project whose last commit was 80 hours ago
acceptance_criteria.then: an alert 'No commits in 80h' is visible for that project in both CLI and web interfaces

user_stories.i_want: to see test pass/fail counts from the last verify run
user_stories.so_that: I know which projects have failing tests without running aitri verify-run manually

acceptance_criteria.given: a project with 04_TEST_RESULTS.json showing passed:28, failed:2, total:30
acceptance_criteria.then: the project entry shows 'Tests: 28/30' and a 'Tests failing (2)' alert
the project entry shows 'Tests: N/A' with no alert for tests

user_stories.as_a: new user with no projects registered
user_stories.i_want: the browser dashboard to guide me to the admin UI instead of showing an empty or broken screen
user_stories.so_that: I can get from zero to my first registered project in one click

acceptance_criteria.given: projects.json is empty (or missing) and the user opens localhost:3000
acceptance_criteria.then: an onboarding panel is visible with title 'No projects yet' and a primary CTA 'Add your first project'

acceptance_criteria.when: the user clicks the primary CTA
acceptance_criteria.then: the browser navigates to /admin and the registration form is visible on arrival

user_stories.i_want: to open a web dashboard at localhost:3000 after one command
user_stories.so_that: I can share the URL with my team for a shared live view

acceptance_criteria.given: Docker is installed and `aitri-hub web` is executed
acceptance_criteria.then: http://localhost:3000 returns HTTP 200 and renders all registered project cards

acceptance_criteria.given: the web dashboard is open in a browser at 768px viewport width
acceptance_criteria.then: all project cards are visible without horizontal scroll and alert badges have contrast ratio ≥4.5:1

user_stories.i_want: to see actionable alerts for projects that need attention
user_stories.so_that: I can prioritize which project to work on without reading raw state files

acceptance_criteria.given: a project with verifyPassed:false and last commit 90 hours ago
acceptance_criteria.then: two alerts are shown: 'Verify failed' and 'No commits in 90h'

acceptance_criteria.given: a project with all phases approved, verifyPassed:true, no drift, and a commit within the last 24h
acceptance_criteria.then: zero alerts are shown for that project

user_stories.i_want: to read aggregated project data from a local JSON file
user_stories.so_that: the React frontend does not need direct filesystem access to individual project directories

acceptance_criteria.then: it contains valid JSON with an array where each entry has name, phases, verifyStatus, gitMeta, testSummary, and alerts fields
the file contains the previous valid state (atomic write ensures no partial corruption)

non_functional_requirements.requirement: Collection cycle for up to 20 local projects must complete within 5 seconds
non_functional_requirements.acceptance_criteria: Running the collector against 20 local projects with valid .aitri files completes in ≤5000ms measured from start to dashboard.json write

non_functional_requirements.requirement: A single unreadable or missing project must not crash the monitor or web server
non_functional_requirements.acceptance_criteria: Given one project with a missing .aitri file among N registered projects, the monitor continues running and all other projects display correctly; error is logged to ~/.aitri-hub/logs/aitri-hub.log

non_functional_requirements.requirement: First-project registration must be completable by a developer with no prior aitri-hub knowledge
non_functional_requirements.acceptance_criteria: A developer unfamiliar with aitri-hub runs `aitri-hub web`, opens localhost:3000, clicks the onboarding CTA, and registers their first project through the /admin form in ≤15 minutes without consulting external documentation

non_functional_requirements.requirement: Web dashboard must work on macOS, Linux, and Windows (WSL2) with Node.js 18+
non_functional_requirements.acceptance_criteria: `aitri-hub web` serves localhost:3000 correctly on macOS 13+, Ubuntu 22.04+, and Windows 11 WSL2 with Node.js ≥18.0.0 installed

non_functional_requirements.requirement: No project data is transmitted outside the local machine
non_functional_requirements.acceptance_criteria: Network traffic analysis during a full monitor session shows zero outbound connections except git pull for remote-registered projects

constraints: Node.js 18+ for the web dashboard; Docker is optional (packaged deployment only)
Zero npm runtime dependencies for the Node server — Node.js built-ins only (mirrors Aitri's design principle)
All persistent data written under ~/.aitri-hub/ — no writes to project directories
Admin HTTP API is localhost-only — loopback peer guard on all /api/* routes, no auth
technology_preferences: Web: React + Vite, bundled to docker/web-dist/ and served by the Node process
Data exchange: JSON files under ~/.aitri-hub/ (projects.json, dashboard.json)
